title: Death and Rebirth of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Death and Rebirth of Evangelion             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Oct 18, 2007 3:58 am what is the point of that movie. the 1st half is all review of the show and the 2nd half is like a preview to EoE. so unless i got screwed when i orders it off eBay (which i highly doubt because it came with EoE and that turned out just fine), what the hell? o and stop me now if im being a complete idiot :P

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Oct 18, 2007 5:48 am It was a bit of a teaser for EoE, as well as a method for including new scenes that are now available in the Platinum Collection. Another one of those quick bucks for those making it, seeing as how it's practically worthless now.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 18, 2007 11:26 am I got the DVD of D&R in the post on Tuesday, haven't watched it yet, but I'm glad I go it, I think. There's lots of extras that I'm looking forward. I watched the first 10 or so minutes of 'Death' and I have to say that I really enjoyed it, especially the music. It's just what I expected and wanted from something like 'Death', to be honest. The quality of the animation seemed better than even the Platnium version, too. Eva Yojimbo wrote: It was a bit of a teaser for EoE, as well as a method for including new scenes that are now available in the Platinum Collection. Another one of those quick bucks for those making it, seeing as how it's practically worthless now.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 18, 2007 11:30 am CitizenGeek wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: It was a bit of a teaser for EoE, as well as a method for including new scenes that are now available in the Platinum Collection. Another one of those quick bucks for those making it, seeing as how it's practically worthless now.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 18, 2007 11:38 am Hexon.Arq wrote: The only thing I remember from it was the shot of Adam fused with Gendo's hand, as well as Lilith's "oops" legs. Come to think of it, those were the only reasons I bought it; I had heard that such minor details were disclosed in the film and was expecting "moments" perhaps devoted to them. There were moments, alright--moments as long as the space between this sentence and its period.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Thu Oct 18, 2007 12:18 pm That's a non-canon scene with Shinji, Rei, Asuka and Kaworu playing Pachelbels Canon.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 18, 2007 6:40 pm There is also the reflection of Kaworu in Rei I's eyes as she's being choked. I don't think it's completely pointless. I really like the way the narrative was weaved together by Tsurimaki. It's, for lack of a better word, very Eva-ish, question-answer, call-response, type thing. Rebirth however, IS completely useless. It's just the first part of Air and NOT the proto-unfinished version of Air that was shown in the theatres as Rebirth.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Oct 19, 2007 6:08 am Well, my personal opinion: Death and Rebirth: when I first watched it, I felt odd at first. Was I watching a recap? But it wasn't just a clip show: there was something about it that was appealing. It was a recap, yet it was interesting. I liked the Death aspect of D&R. It made me feel warmer inside to see the characters one last time before the end of the world. Rebirth, pointless. It was just an unfinished part of the next movie, so I don't see much point to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 19, 2007 6:15 am Wevangeliwon wrote: It was just an unfinished part of the next movie, so I don't see much point to it.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 19, 2007 1:14 pm I'm going to be investigating this in full later, but as far as is my understanding, OTHER than what later became Director's Cut scenes, etc....and some minor retouches to some other scenes which were purely matters of refinement of artwork, the ONLY new thing in Rebirth was explicitly pointing out that Adam is fused to Gendo's hand?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 19, 2007 1:23 pm V wrote: I'm going to be investigating this in full later, but as far as is my understanding, OTHER than what later became Director's Cut scenes, etc....and some minor retouches to some other scenes which were purely matters of refinement of artwork, the ONLY new thing in Rebirth was explicitly pointing out that Adam is fused to Gendo's hand?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:58 am Ornette wrote: I don't think it's completely pointless. I really like the way the narrative was weaved together by Tsurimaki. It's, for lack of a better word, very Eva-ish, question-answer, call-response, type thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Sat Oct 20, 2007 6:13 pm Ok folks, let's tell him the truth. D&R is for THE MONEY ( Where's the money?! Where's the money?! ) and Tamashi no Rufuran. Srly.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:04 pm Death & Rebirth is more an oddity to me. At the very least I'm glad I didn't have to wait to see it (being about oh a decade late to the Eva show) cause any anticipation for the movie would've been extremely frustrating. The DEATH part of the movie is actually quite good. It's more experimental than anything else going through the series in a highly non-chronological way trying to give people the opportunity to create their own hypothesis on what each character signifies and wants. It's also got the most goofy non-canon scene in the series when Kaworu comes in to play musical instruments with them. Cause if it was any way true that would mean Kaworu had been around for a couple months and we know Shinji would've gone all the way in that case. DEATH mostly acts as a cool way to think about the individual characters and whats the point of the whole series. Plus, to also see the best looking interpretations of key scenes (while I haven't seen Eva 1.0 yet, the trailers lead me to believe that Shinji's scene with Rei post Ramiel is best animated here in DEATH...plus the reanimated Misato/Shinji scene is wicked too). REBIRTH- can't sit it through it again. Pure cock tease. Imagine my frustration when I was first watching it, not knowing what it was, it ends and I get all psyched for EOE and then I pop the disc in and.....its the same shit.....only a few changes here and there.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:15 pm Wait, the scene where Rei smiles at Shinji that was redone, was that also redone in the "Director's Cut" (or whatever you wish to call them)? Mailny because... I haven't seen them.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:28 pm Barinax wrote: Wait, the scene where Rei smiles at Shinji that was redone, was that also redone in the "Director's Cut" (or whatever you wish to call them)? Mailny because... I haven't seen them.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:31 pm I actually prefer the TV version because her smile is bigger. Yeah, I know, I'm a weirdo.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:33 pm Is she even smiling in the Death version?

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:35 pm I agree. Quality might be better, but I preferred the first smile. The Director's cuts only cover episodes 21-24

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:39 pm I may have taken the screenshot a little too early. It was done in haste. Barinax wrote: Really? Err, maybe that was Renewal. I think it was only released in Japan and had more redone scenes... such as Rei's room... containing dolls, apparently, from what I've heard.
